commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=14beb939baeca09a0b2fe932887fe78d1269fc05)
commands.review_summary:
The PR splits official org handles into legacy and reservation-verified sets, gates `nvidia` Official status on reserved-owner org ownership, blocks unreserved self-serve creation of such handles, and updates tests/specs.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows `isOfficialPublisher` returns true for the `nvidia` org handle without checking reservations, and the pre-PR test expected that behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is an owner/security rollout decision and production data proof, not a narrow code repair.

target.title: fix(official): require verified ownership for newly allowlisted official org handles
target.body:
### Motivation

- A new allowlist entry (e.g. `nvidia`) made Official status rely solely on the mutable publisher handle, which allowed handle-squatting and unauthorized Official package publishing. 
- The change must bind Official status to a verified owner/reservation so unclaimed or attacker-created orgs cannot acquire Official privileges by registering the handle.

### Description

- Add reserved-owner verification and a dedicated org-level check by introducing `isReservedOwnerVerifiedOfficialOrgHandle` and `isOfficialOrgPublisher` and consult them from `isOfficialPublisher` in `convex/lib/officialPublishers.ts`.
- Require an active `reservedHandles` record whose `rightfulOwnerUserId` owns the org before treating a newly allowlisted handle (e.g. `nvidia`) as Official, and require the same verification when deriving personal publisher Official status from org membership.
- Block self-serve org creation for reserved-owner-verified official handles in `createOrgPublisherForUser` unless the handle is already reserved for the actor by checking `getLatestActiveReservedHandle` in `convex/publishers.ts`.
- Adjust `convex/lib/reservedHandles.ts` to expose a `DbCtx`-friendly `getLatestActiveReservedHandle` helper and add regression tests and a spec update (`specs/official-publishers.md`, `convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts`, `convex/publishers.test.ts`).

### Testing

- Ran targeted tests with `bunx vitest run convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts convex/publishers.test.ts --reporter verbose` and `bunx vitest run convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts convex/publishers.test.ts convex/users.test.ts --reporter verbose`, which passed.
- Ran broader checks `bun run ci:unit` and `bun run ci:types-build`, both of which completed successfully in this environment.
- Formatting and lint checks `bun run format:check && bun run lint` completed successfully, while `bun run ci:static` and `bun run deadcode:ci` could not fully run in this environment due to `bun audit` and `knip`/registry access issues respectively, which are environment/tooling limits and not regressions in the change.
- Attempted local autoreview (`.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview`) but the container lacked the configured reviewer CLI, so autoreview could not run here.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_6a1a8005439883279288023660d5dc04)
target.author: bunsdev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
